standardization: train-fold mean and population standard deviation; zero-variance scale set to 1
initialization: deterministic mean-nearest first centroid, then farthest-first
recessionClusterMapping: among train clusters containing at least one NBER recession month, select the single cluster with highest Laplace-smoothed recession rate; deterministic tie breaks
uniqueDateAggregation: earliest fold prediction for dates present in overlapping test windows
selectionPolicy: configuration fixed before test evaluation; no hyperparameter sweep
limitations: Cluster identity is not a macro-regime taxonomy and is mapped only to a binary recession task.
Each train window contains very few NBER recession months; cluster-to-label mapping is unstable by construction.
The five inputs are normalized features from the retrospective baseline evaluation, not raw real-time vintage feature engineering.
No automatic promotion is allowed; comparison requires the human Model Gate.
